exemplar.title: Video Documentary Depicting the Road to Recovery from an injury (French)

detailedExemplarData.title: Video Documentary Depicting the Road to Recovery from an injury (French)

strengths: Concrete evidence of using scheduling tools to balance classes and training, showing effective time-management ATL skills
Multiple detailed examples of creative thinking to develop innovative training exercises and enhance video backgrounds
Clear descriptions of how creative thinking solved production challenges and added new features, underlining impact on product quality
weaknesses: One example of creative thinking lacks specific evidence of its impact on performance metrics
Opportunities to quantify efficiency gains (e.g., reduced editing time) when applying ATL skills are not fully exploited
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: **The student provides rich, detailed evidence of ATL skill application**, particularly in creative thinking and organization, which directly improves both their learning process and product quality. To elevate this further, **they should quantify the impact of these skills** (for instance, by citing measurable performance improvements or time savings) to demonstrate effectiveness more robustly.

strengths: Clear articulation of a personal learning goal rooted in the studentâs passion for football, providing strong intrinsic motivation
A wellâstructured timeline that lists key tasks and dates, demonstrating an organized approach to planning
Comprehensive task list showing commitment and a logical sequence of production quality steps
weaknesses: Success criteria are not measurable and rely solely on subjective audience opinions
Lack of detailed resource planning (e.g., equipment availability, software licensing) to ensure feasibility
Timeline omits assigned responsibilities and realistic time estimates for each task
Extended task list does not integrate feedback loops or reflection checkpoints to monitor progress
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: **The planning section demonstrates a genuine personal connection and a thoughtful sequence of tasks**, reflecting clear organization and commitment. However, **the success criteria need to be made measurable with objective indicators**, and more detail is required on resources, task ownership, and built-in review stages to strengthen accountability and project feasibility.

strengths: Thorough aesthetic evaluation against success criteria, supported by 100% positive audience feedback on readability and production quality
Insightful reflection on accessibility decisions (subtitles placement), demonstrating empathy for the audience
Clear evidence of personal growth: increased motivation, accountability, self-awareness, and acquisition of technical filming and editing skills
weaknesses: Reflection could be deepened by critically examining any unmet criteria or by proposing specific future adjustments based on lessons learned
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: **The reflective section is a strong showcase of evaluative maturity,** blending concrete audience data with personal insights into learning and skill development. To reach an even higher level, **the student could include more critical analysis of areas that fell short and outline targeted steps for future improvement.**

annotations.comment: You articulate your learning goal clearly but you have not defined measurable success criteria to assess when you have achieved this goal. Consider adding specific indicators (e.g., number of documented sessions, peer feedback ratings) to make your criteria measurable.

You establish a strong, personal connection between your interest in football and your learning goal. This choice of personal context will motivate your work and make your video more authentic to the intended audience.

Your organization example clearly shows use of scheduling tools to manage time between classes and training. This concrete evidence highlights effective ATL skill application toward achieving your learning goal.

Your success criteria table is comprehensive but relies solely on subjective audience opinions. Consider incorporating objective metrics (e.g., readability scores or time-on-screen thresholds) to strengthen your assessment methods.

Your example of explorations in creative thinking lacks specific evidence of impact. For instance, detail how a particular exercise improved performance metrics to illustrate the effectiveness of the skill.
Describing how you used creative thinking to help peers avoid mistakes effectively shows the broader impact of your ATL skills. This evidence substantiates the usefulness of your reflections.

Your plan identifies key production quality steps but lacks details on resource requirements (e.g., equipment availability, software licensing). Specify these to ensure feasibility and avoid last-minute sourcing issues.

The timeline lists tasks and dates clearly but omits assigned responsibilities or effort estimates. Add who will complete each task and approximate time allocations to improve accountability and scheduling realism.

You provide detailed examples of using creative thinking to find innovative training exercises, demonstrating effective application of ATL skills to your learning goal. This strengthens your self-directed learning evidence.

Your extended task list shows commitment but stops before integrating feedback loops or reflection checkpoints. Embed review stages to assess progress against criteria and adjust your plan mid-project.

You explain challenges encountered during video production and how creative thinking helped you overcome them, showing clear impact on product quality. Consider quantifying efficiency gains (e.g., reduced editing time).

Your solution to enhance video backgrounds with creative techniques illustrates a strong application of ATL skills in product development. This example underlines the direct impact on production quality.
Adding new features through creative ideation demonstrates how you extended your product's functionality. This detailed example effectively shows ATL skills contributing to a richer final outcome.
Your aesthetic reflections align your video choices with success criteria and target audience preferences, demonstrating thorough evaluation of the final product against intended standards.
Your decision to place subtitles in a black bar shows attention to accessibility and audience needs, reinforcing that you considered multiple criteria when evaluating the product.
Reporting that 100% of your audience found the video aesthetically pleasing and text readable provides strong evidence of meeting your success criteria and supports your reflective evaluation.
Reflecting on how documenting your progress increased your motivation and accountability gives clear insight into the personal impact of the project on your learning journey.
Your reflection on self-awareness and identifying areas for improvement demonstrates a deep evaluation of your learning process, showing reflective maturity.
Discussing the acquisition of new technical skills (filming, editing) highlights tangible learning outcomes. This evidence effectively demonstrates the impact on your skill development.
Your evaluation of production quality against professional equipment criteria, with 100% positive audience feedback, solidly confirms the product met your fabrication standards.
